title: UV Layer Workflow Rewrite — Unified Coordinate System
context: Rewrite the UV layer workflow to use a single coordinate system (winder command positions) for all calibration artifacts instead of camera coordinates + offsets. This improves consistency, reduces transformation complexity, and adds backlash correction. See docs/UVlayerRewritePlan.md for full specification.

tasks.title: Add pin naming utilities (tangent_sides, face ranges, endpoint pins)
tasks.description:
Implement the pin naming convention and derived calculations per the spec. Create a new module (or extend geometry.py) with utility functions for:
- tangent_sides(layer, side, n) -> tuple[int, int]
- Determine face from pin number (head, bottom, foot, top)
- ENDPOINT_PINS list for both U and V
- Pin name formatting (e.g., 'UA1' format)

Add comprehensive tests for all pin ranges and edge cases.
acceptance_criteria: tangent_sides() returns correct x,y orientation for all pin combinations
Face determination works correctly for all 4 faces in U and V layers
ENDPOINT_PINS includes all endpoint pins per spec
Pin name formatting supports both 'UA1' and numeric formats
All public functions have test coverage including boundary cases

tasks.title: Update calibration file format to store raw camera positions
tasks.description:
Modify the calibration artifact format to store raw camera coordinates without pre-applying the camera-wire offset. The offset will be applied by the interpreter instead.

Update:
- Calibration JSON schema
- Calibration recording in layer_calibration.py / manual_calibration.py
- Documentation about the new format

Include backlash correction in recorded values (detect reversal window consumption and record actual space position).
acceptance_criteria: New calibration files store raw camera positions (no offset applied)
Backlash correction is applied during recording
Old calibration format can still be read (migration path exists)
Calibration schema is documented
Tests verify recorded positions match expected raw camera coordinates

tasks.title: Implement coordinate system transformer (camera -> command)
tasks.description:
Create a coordinator that transforms raw camera coordinates to winder command positions using:
- Camera-wire offset (from machine calibration)
- Arm correction (head shift signs, tangent y side)
- Machine parameters (roller y calibration)

This replaces the logic currently scattered in ~anchorToTarget macro and gcode interpreters. Should be a pure function with no side effects.
acceptance_criteria: Transformer accepts raw camera position, layer, side, pin number, and machine params
Returns winder command position
Arm correction applied correctly per spec
Camera-wire offset applied correctly
Tests compare output against known good coordinate transformations

tasks.title: Update wire solver (anchorToTarget equivalent) to use new coordinates
tasks.description:
Rewrite the wire solver that computes the wire path between two pins. The solver must:
- Accept two raw camera positions (anchor and target pins)
- Apply tangent orientation per tangent_sides()
- Return winder command positions for both ends
- Ensure wire is tangent to both pins at correct orientation

This replaces the ~anchorToTarget macro logic. Should integrate with the coordinate transformer from task 3.
acceptance_criteria: Solver correctly computes wire endpoints for all pin pairs
Tangent orientation is correct for each face and side
Output winder command positions match expected values
Solver accepts machine params (camera offset, arm correction, rollers)
Tests cover multiple pin pairs on different faces

tasks.title: Update gcode interpreter to consume new calibration format
tasks.description:
Update the gcode handler/interpreter to:
- Read new calibration files (raw camera positions)
- Apply coordinate transformation using the transformer from task 3
- Support ~anchorToTarget with raw pin names (e.g., 'UA1')
- Support increment, goto, and legacy G... P... commands with new coordinates

Must maintain backward compatibility with legacy G... P... commands if still needed.
acceptance_criteria: Gcode interpreter reads new calibration file format
~anchorToTarget command works with new pin names
increment and goto commands work with new coordinates
Legacy G... P... commands still work (if needed)
All coordinate transformations use unified system
Tests verify gcode playback produces correct winder commands

tasks.title: Update calibration UI (Calibrate.html / calibrate.py) for new flow
tasks.description:
Update the calibration UI to use the new workflow:
- Record raw camera positions (no pre-applied offset)
- Apply backlash correction during recording
- Generate new calibration file format
- Display new pin naming scheme (Layer/Side/Number)

Update any supporting Python code (calibrate.py).
acceptance_criteria: UI displays pin names in new format (e.g., 'UA1')
Calibration recording captures raw camera positions
Backlash correction is applied
Generated calibration files match new format
UI provides feedback on calibration quality

tasks.title: Migrate existing calibration data and add compatibility layer
tasks.description:
Create migration utilities to:
- Convert old calibration files (with pre-applied offsets) to new format
- Add a compatibility layer that reads old format and converts on-the-fly
- Run migration on all existing calibration artifacts
- Verify converted data matches expected raw camera positions

This allows gradual rollout — old calibrations still work while new ones use unified system.
acceptance_criteria: Migration script converts old format to new format correctly
Compatibility layer reads old format transparently
Converted calibrations are verified against source data
All existing test calibrations successfully migrated
No data loss during migration

tasks.title: Integration test: end-to-end calibration and winding flow
tasks.description:
Create integration tests that verify the complete flow:
1. Record calibration (new format, with backlash correction)
2. Load calibration and wire solver
3. Plan wire paths between pins using solver
4. Generate gcode with new pin names
5. Execute gcode and verify winder commands
6. Compare against legacy system (if available) to ensure accuracy

Should test multiple wire patterns and pin combinations.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end flow completes without errors
Winder commands match expected values for test pins
Multiple wire patterns are tested
Results are consistent with legacy system (within tolerance)
Test data is committed for regression testing
